methodsAttempted.query: site:utdallas.edu generative AI policy students teaching guidance University of Texas at Dallas
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search returned the central GenAI hub, Information Security AI guidelines, OIT GenAI page, and CTL teaching article.
Firecrawl search surfaced the formal Policy Navigator page UTDSP5017.
Search found course-specific syllabi and a news item; these were recorded as rejected or context because they were not preferred university-wide sources.

artifacts.summary: Official UT Dallas sources were available. The crawl selected the Policy Navigator page, central GenAI hub, OIT GenAI tools page, OIT Initial Guidelines, and Information Security AI usage guidelines as claim evidence; lower-specificity teaching articles, KB overview material, and course-specific syllabi were rejected or treated as context.

artifacts.sourceTitle: UTDSP5017 Generative AI Use in Academic Work

artifacts.claimText: UT Dallas policy says faculty have discretion to permit, prohibit, or partially allow generative AI use in academic work under faculty supervision.

artifacts.claimText: UT Dallas policy says faculty must explain and communicate appropriate generative AI use before academic work begins for a given semester, and should include detailed statements in assignment descriptions and syllabi.

artifacts.sourceTitle: UTDSP5017 Generative AI Use in Academic Work
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Students should document and attribute the use of generative AI as appropriate to the academic style (APA, MLA, Chicago, etc.) or a professional style specified by the faculty member. Students must follow written guidelines from faculty on citation styles. Students must validate or verify the output from generative AI.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Students should document and attribute permitted generative AI use, follow written citation-style guidance, and validate or verify generative AI output.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: When performing official UT Dallas duties, it is recommended that faculty and staff use AI tools and AI software-as-a-service (Saas) websites provided by UT Dallas, rather than obtaining personal access to public or vended AI platforms because these are not approved and have not yet been evaluated for security.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: For official duties, faculty and staff are recommended to use UT Dallas-provided AI tools before personal public or vended AI platforms that have not been approved or security-evaluated.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Always Use UT Dallas Approved AI Tools First

artifacts.sourceTitle: Initial Guidelines for Gen AI | OIT | UT Dallas
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: You should not enter data classified as confidential into publicly-available Generative AI tools, following the University’s Information Security Policy. Information shared with Generative AI tools using default settings is not private and could expose proprietary or sensitive information to unauthorized parties. No FERPA or HIPAA information may be used in Generative AI tools.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: OIT guidance says confidential data should not be entered into publicly available generative AI tools and that FERPA or HIPAA information may not be used in generative AI tools.

artifacts.notes: Claim is directly supported by UTDSP5017 and scoped to academic work under faculty supervision.
The claim preserves the policy's must language for faculty communication before academic work begins.
The claim is limited to permitted generative AI use and follows the policy's documentation and validation wording.
The claim is scoped to faculty and staff performing official duties and uses recommended language rather than a requirement.
The claim is directly supported by OIT guidance on confidential, FERPA, and HIPAA information.
The claim is a source-status statement about OIT's GenAI tools page, not an approval claim beyond the page text.

artifacts.title: University of Texas at Dallas Generative AI Policy Evidence
artifacts.summary: Staged evidence for UT Dallas identifies a formal Generative AI Use in Academic Work policy, central GenAI hub, OIT GenAI tools resources, OIT initial guidance, and Information Security AI usage guidelines. Claims are limited to faculty discretion and communication, student attribution and verification, official-duty tool guidance, and confidential or regulated data handling.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
This staged bundle does not publish canonical claims and does not mark any record human_reviewed.
Course-specific syllabi and webinar-style guidance were not used for university-wide policy claims.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
